data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Make setup CLI additive (Revision 7)

## Prior Rejection Gaps — All Addressed

| # | Gap | Fix in this plan |
|---|-----|------------------|
| 1 | TOML parser only handled shell-writer format | Step 1: state-machine reader handles both shell-writer triple-quoted and smol-toml escaped single-line formats |
| 2 | REGISTERED_AGENTS=() resets not removed | Step 4: deletes setup.sh:930 and agent-wizard.sh:499 explicitly |
| 3 | Dispatch editing not additive | Steps 7-8: show current mode/mappings/pools with Keep current defaults |
| 4 | Detected agents filtered by existing registrations | Step 4c: FOUND_AGENTS never filtered; all detected agents shown; model menus show all available models |
| 5 | Tests lacked interactive verification | Step 12B-C: PTY-driven tests that seed settings, run setup, add one item, assert everything else preserved |
| 6 | backend.type, defaults.profileId, terminalLightTheme lost | Steps 1 and 10: reader loads all scalars; writer reads from KV store |
| 7 | Agent provider/flavor/version fields dropped | Steps 1 and 10d: reader stores and writer outputs per-agent provider/flavor/version |

## Architecture

Three layers: (1) pure-bash TOML reader populates existing KV store from settings.toml on startup, (2) wizard UX changes show current state and default to keeping it, (3) writer fixes read pass-through fields from KV instead of hardcoding defaults.

Reader handles two TOML formats: shell-writer (triple-quoted multiline) and smol-toml (escaped single-line with literal \n).

## Step 1: Add _read_existing_settings() to setup.sh

Location: After KV helpers (after line 162), before _slugify() (line 164). Pure-bash state-machine TOML reader (~110 lines). Reads $_AGENT_SETTINGS_FILE line by line. No external dependencies.

State machine states: top (scalars), agents (inside [agents.<id>]), actions, backend, defaults, scope (inside [scopeRefinement]), scope_multiline (triple-quoted body), pools_inline ([pools] with step = []), pools_aot ([[pools.<step>]] with agentId/weight).

Populates: REGISTERED_AGENTS array, _kv_set AGENT_COMMANDS/LABELS/MODELS/PROVIDERS/FLAVORS/VERSIONS per agent, _kv_set ACTION_MAP, _kv_set BACKEND type, _kv_set DEFAULTS profileId/max_concurrent_sessions/max_claims_per_queue_type, _kv_set DISPATCH dispatch_mode, _TERMINAL_LIGHT_THEME variable, _SCOPE_PROMPT variable, _kv_set POOL_COUNT/POOL_AGENT_*/POOL_WEIGHT_* for advanced pools.

Handles: section headers, string/int/bool values, triple-quoted multiline, escaped single-line strings (printf %b for \n), empty arrays. Returns 0 if settings file missing.

## Step 2: Add identical _read_existing_settings() to agent-wizard.sh

Same logic referencing $SETTINGS_FILE. Loads ALL settings so writer preserves them.

## Step 3: Make _register_agent_entry() dedup-aware

Files: setup.sh:761-767, agent-wizard.sh:324-330. Check if ID already in REGISTERED_AGENTS before appending. If exists, update metadata via _kv_set without re-appending. If new, append normally.

## Step 4: Remove REGISTERED_AGENTS=() resets; keep all detected agents visible

4a. Delete REGISTERED_AGENTS=() at setup.sh:930 and agent-wizard.sh:499.

4b. When REGISTERED_AGENTS has entries from reader, show them before scanning: "Currently configured agents: claude-opus-4, codex-gpt-5 / Scan for additional agents? [Y/n]". Fresh install: original prompt unchanged.

4c. Do NOT filter FOUND_AGENTS. All detected agent commands always shown. If codex is registered with variant codex-gpt-5 and codex is detected, user sees full codex model menu and can add codex-gpt-5-2 alongside existing variant.

4d. Single-agent case: register with dedup (Step 3). Only assign action mappings for actions that have no current mapping (_kv_get returns empty).

## Step 5: Update _prompt_action_choice() to show current value

Files: setup.sh:726-748, agent-wizard.sh:289-311. Add current_agent parameter. Show (current) marker next to matching agent. Default selection is current agent index. Enter keeps existing mapping. Fresh install: behavior unchanged.

## Step 6: Make _prompt_action_mappings() pass current values

Files: setup.sh:879-894, agent-wizard.sh:442-456. Read _kv_get ACTION_MAP for each action, pass as current_agent to _prompt_action_choice.

## Step 7: Make _dispatch_wizard() show Keep current option

File: setup.sh:1020-1047. When config exists: 1) Keep current (recommended), 2) Simple, 3) Advanced [default 1]. Fresh install: original 2-choice menu.

## Step 8: Make _prompt_single_pool() additive

File: setup.sh:962-1003. When pool has entries: show current entries, offer 1) Keep current (recommended), 2) Reconfigure, 0) Clear pool. Only this pool affected; others untouched.

## Step 9: Make _defaults_wizard() use current values as defaults

File: setup.sh:1125-1141. Read current from KV store instead of hardcoded 5/10. Prompt shows current value as default. Scope prompt already reads from _SCOPE_PROMPT.

## Step 10: Update _write_settings_toml() in both scripts

10a. backend.type from _kv_get BACKEND type "auto" (not hardcoded).
10b. defaults.profileId from _kv_get DEFAULTS profileId "" (not hardcoded).
10c. Add terminalLightTheme = ${_TERMINAL_LIGHT_THEME:-false} after maxClaimsPerQueueType.
10d. Per-agent provider/flavor/version from KV when non-empty.
10e. agent-wizard.sh: full pool writer (same as setup.sh:669-709) replacing empty-pools-only writer.

## Step 11: Wire up entry points

11a. setup.sh foolery_setup(): call _read_existing_settings before while loop. Mark steps [configured] in menu when settings loaded.
11b. agent-wizard.sh maybe_agent_wizard(): call _read_existing_settings before scan prompt.

## Step 12: Tests

### A. Unit tests in setup-settings-writer.test.ts (8 new tests)

1. reader seeds agents into REGISTERED_AGENTS
2. reader seeds all scalar settings (non-default values)
3. reader seeds scope prompt (triple-quoted format)
4. reader seeds scope prompt (smol-toml escaped format)
5. reader seeds pool config (advanced mode [[pools.step]])
6. load-then-write round-trip preserves all settings
7. preserves existing agents when adding new ones (seed 2 + add 1 = 3)
8. dedup on re-register (same ID, updated model = 1 entry)

### B. PTY test: scripts/test-setup-additive.sh

Follows test-install-copilot-setup.sh pattern. Seed: 2 agents (claude-opus-4, codex-gpt-5), action mappings, backend.type=knots, defaults.profileId=guided, terminalLightTheme=true, maxConcurrentSessions=8, maxClaimsPerQueueType=15, custom scope prompt.

Run 1: Add claude-sonnet-4 variant via Agents wizard. Assert all existing preserved + new variant added. Assert all scalars/backend/defaults/scope/mappings unchanged.

Run 2: Change one dispatch mapping. Assert all agents present, change applied, everything else unchanged.

### C. PTY test: scripts/test-agent-wizard-additive.sh

Seed 2 agents + non-default backend/defaults. Run maybe_agent_wizard, add variant. Assert all settings preserved.

### D. Register in package.json as test:smoke:setup:additive and test:smoke:wizard:additive

## Complexity: Medium

~200 lines new bash, ~100 lines bash modifications, ~400 lines tests. Risk: bash TOML parsing mitigated by round-trip tests and constrained format. No new deps. Fresh installs unchanged.

## File Changes

| File | Type | Lines |
|------|------|-------|
| scripts/setup.sh | Modified | +200 |
| scripts/agent-wizard.sh | Modified | +160 |
| setup-settings-writer.test.ts | Modified | +200 |
| test-setup-additive.sh | New | ~120 |
| test-agent-wizard-additive.sh | New | ~80 |
| package.json | Modified | +2 |
